title: How do I open an account?
text: You can open a personal account entirely from the mobile app. You will need a valid government-issued ID and a smartphone for identity verification. The process has four steps: confirm your email and personal details, choose a personal or business account, complete identity verification, and add funds via bank transfer. Verification is available in several languages including English and German. Most accounts are ready within minutes once verification succeeds.

title: What account types are available?
text: We offer a free standard personal account and several paid membership tiers that add benefits such as travel insurance, higher withdrawal limits, and metal cards. Business and freelancer accounts are also available; to open a freelancer account you first open a personal account. Each tier lists its monthly price and included features in the app before you choose.

title: What is my IBAN and where do I find it?
text: Your IBAN (International Bank Account Number) is the identifier used to send and receive euro payments. You can find it in the app under your account details, and on your account statements. A German IBAN is 22 characters long and begins with the country code DE followed by a two-digit check number. Share your IBAN with anyone who needs to send you a SEPA transfer.

title: How long does a SEPA transfer take?
text: A standard SEPA credit transfer in euro is typically credited to the recipient within one business day. Transfers submitted on weekends or public holidays are processed on the next business day. SEPA Instant transfers, where supported by both banks, arrive within seconds. SEPA transfers can only be made in euro; payments in other currencies use a separate international transfer process.

title: Can I get a refund on a SEPA direct debit?
text: For an authorised SEPA Core direct debit, you can request a refund within eight weeks of the date your account was debited, and the amount is credited back to you. If the direct debit was collected without a valid mandate (an unauthorised payment), you can demand a refund for up to 13 months after the debit. You can request a refund from within the app or by contacting support.

title: My card is lost or stolen — what do I do?
text: Freeze your card immediately from the app to block any further use. If you cannot access the app, contact support to have the card blocked. Once blocked, you can order a replacement card from the app; a delivery estimate is shown at the time you order. If you suspect fraudulent transactions, report them so they can be investigated and disputed.

title: Are there fees for using my card abroad?
text: Card payments in euro are generally free on all account tiers. Payments in foreign currencies may include a small exchange-rate-based fee on standard accounts, while some paid tiers waive this fee. ATM cash withdrawals are free up to a monthly limit that depends on your account tier; withdrawals beyond that limit or in foreign currency may incur a charge. Exact limits and fees are listed in the app.

title: Where can I withdraw cash?
text: You can withdraw cash at any ATM that accepts Mastercard or Maestro. The number of free withdrawals per month depends on your account tier. In some countries you can also deposit or withdraw cash at partner retail locations. Each withdrawal shows any applicable fee before you confirm.

title: How is my account secured?
text: Your account is protected by app-based two-factor authentication linked to your smartphone. Sensitive actions such as payments and changing personal data require confirmation on your paired device. We never ask for your password or PIN by phone or email. If your phone is lost, contact support to secure your account and re-pair a new device.

title: How do I change my phone number or address?
text: You can update your address in the app under personal details. Changing your phone number is a security-sensitive action: it requires identity verification, because your phone is used for two-factor authentication. If your old phone is lost or broken and you cannot verify, contact support and they will guide you through re-verifying your identity before the change is applied.

title: Why is there a hold on my deposit?
text: Incoming SEPA transfers are usually available as soon as they are booked. Occasionally a deposit may be held briefly for security or verification reasons, especially for large amounts or a first transfer from a new sender. Holds are temporary; if a deposit is held longer than expected, contact support with the transfer details.

title: How do I close my account?
text: You can request account closure from the app or by contacting support. Before closing, move any remaining balance to another account and cancel active direct debits and standing orders so payments are not rejected. Once closed, you will no longer be able to log in or access historic statements, so download any records you need beforehand.

title: What is the difference between a standing order and a direct debit?
text: A standing order is an instruction you set up to send a fixed amount to the same recipient on a regular schedule; you control it. A direct debit is an authorisation you give a company to collect variable amounts from your account, for example a utility bill. You can cancel a standing order at any time in the app, and you can revoke a direct debit mandate to stop future collections.

title: How do I contact support?
text: You can reach support through in-app chat during published hours, and an automated assistant is available around the clock for common questions. Phone support may be limited to certain paid account tiers. For urgent card blocking, use the app freeze function or the emergency blocking line. When you contact support, include a clear summary and any relevant screenshots to speed up resolution.

title: Do I need a BIC for a transfer?
text: For SEPA transfers within the European Economic Area, you generally only need the recipient's IBAN; the BIC (Business Identifier Code) is derived automatically. For some cross-border transfers outside the EEA, the BIC may still be required in addition to the IBAN. The app will tell you which fields are needed when you set up a transfer.
